service: Shape the use and development of New York City to make it more equitable, prosperous, resilient, and sustainable citywide.
oneLiner: This mandate traces back to the 1938 city charter reform, which created the City Planning Commission as an independent body responsible for the whole city's growth, replacing the old system where land use decisions were scattered and unaccountable.
summary: New York City's first modern reform charter, approved in 1936 and effective January 1, 1938, created the City Planning Commission to be, in the charter's own words, an independent commission responsible for the welfare of the entire city, advising on zoning, the city map, capital spending, and a long-range master plan for growth. That founding mandate is the direct ancestor of DCP's current mission to guide equitable, prosperous, resilient and sustainable development citywide.
